About: It is a Korean martial art.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Daitō-ryū Aiki-jūjutsu.
ConsistsOf: It consists of joint locks, grappling, chokeholds, throwing techniques, kicks, punches, and other striking attacks.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Choi Yong-sool's disciples.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Choi Yong-sool.
FoundedIn: It was founded in the post-Japanese colonial period of Korea.
HasA: It has the use of traditional weapons.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by joint locks.
It is characterized by grappling.
It is characterized by chokeholds.
It is characterized by throwing techniques.
It is characterized by kicks.
It is characterized by punches.
It is characterized by striking attacks.
It is characterized by the use of traditional weapons.
It is characterized by circular motion.
It is characterized by redirection of force.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by Daitō-ryū Aiki-jūjutsu.
It was influenced by Tang Soo Do.
IsA: It is a hybrid form of self-defense.
It is a Korean martial art.
KnownAs: It is known as hap ki do.
KnownFor: It is known for employing joint locks.
It is known for employing grappling.
It is known for employing chokeholds.
It is known for employing throwing techniques.
It is known for employing kicks.
It is known for employing punches.
It is known for employing striking attacks.
It is known for teaching the use of traditional weapons.
It is known for employing both long-range and close-range fighting techniques.
It is known for emphasizing circular motion, redirection of force, and control of the opponent.
UsedTo: It is used to gain advantage over opponents through footwork and body positioning.
It is used to incorporate the use of leverage.
It is used to avoid the use of brute strength against brute strength.
